name: Viriato Trail: Sierra de San Vicente Loop
name_original: Cerro Cruces – circular desde El Real de San Vicente por la senda Viriato (GR 63)
description: This demanding 22.9-mile mountain biking route in Sierra de San Vicente offers rugged terrain, significant climbs, and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around El Real De San Vicente offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and open countryside. The region is situated within the Sierra de San Vicente, providing varied terrain with both gradual ascents and more challenging climbs. Riders can expect to encounter scenic viewpoints overlooking valleys and agricultural areas. Best mountain bike trails around El Real De San Vicente The most popular mountain bike trail is Viriato Trail: Sierra de San Vicente Loop, a 22.9 miles (36.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route features challenging climbs through the Sierra de San Vicente. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Puente de Los Pilones – Livestock landscape loop from Castillo de Bayuela, a moderate 25.2 miles (40.6 km) path. This route winds through open livestock landscapes, offering varied terrain and scenic views. Local mountain bikers also love the View of the Tagus Valley – Jurisdictional Pillory loop from Castillo de Bayuela, a 20.2 miles (32.6 km) trail leading through hills and offering expansive views of the Tagus Valley, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Mountain biking around El Real De San Vicente is defined by rolling hills, scenic valleys, and extensive woodlands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various skill levels. The routes in El Real De San Vicente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Real De San Vicente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in El Real De San Vicente?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails around El Real De San Vicente, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The region's diverse landscape of rolling hills and woodlands provides varied terrain for exploration.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in El Real De San Vicente?
A: Yes, El Real De San Vicente offers 5 easy mountain bike trails, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less challenging climbs and more gentle terrain.

Q: What options are there for experienced mountain bikers in El Real De San Vicente?
A: For experienced mountain bikers, there are 24 difficult trails available. These routes often include challenging climbs and descents through the Sierra de San Vicente, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025844632/viriato-trail-sierra-de-san-vicente-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viriato Trail: Sierra de San Vicente Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in El Real De San Vicente?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in El Real De San Vicente are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33841760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Los Pilones – Livestock landscape loop from Castillo de Bayuela&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tagus Valley – Jurisdictional Pillory loop from Castillo de Bayuela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of distances can I expect on mountain bike trails in this region?
A: The mountain bike trails in El Real De San Vicente vary significantly in distance. You can find routes ranging from shorter, more accessible rides to longer, more demanding expeditions. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31494443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Viewpoint – No entry loop from Castillo de Bayuela&lt;/a&gt; is nearly 50 km long, while others are shorter.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in El Real De San Vicente?
A: The mountain bike trails in El Real De San Vicente are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, scenic viewpoints overlooking valleys, and the extensive woodlands that characterize the region.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in El Real De San Vicente?
A: Yes, many trails offer scenic viewpoints. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40382302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tagus Valley – Jurisdictional Pillory loop from Castillo de Bayuela&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive views of the Tagus Valley. The region's rolling hills frequently open up to panoramic vistas.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or places to explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore several interesting landmarks. These include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mosquea&lt;/a&gt;, the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Hill (1320m)&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5450384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rollo de Justicia of Castillo de Bayuela&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2607538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Piélago Pass&lt;/a&gt; also offers notable scenery.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in El Real De San Vicente?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be hot, while winters are generally mild but may have occasional rain, making some trails muddy.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in El Real De San Vicente?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not always signposted, you can typically find parking in or near the villages and towns that serve as starting points for many routes, such as El Real De San Vicente itself or Castillo de Bayuela.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain biking options in the area?
A: While the guide highlights more moderate to difficult trails, the 5 easy trails available in El Real De San Vicente can be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. These routes generally have gentler gradients and less technical terrain.
tips: The summit of Cruces at 1,373 meters above sea level is one of the most impressive panoramic points in the Navamorcuende region. It offers beautiful views in all directions so I recommend taking some time and enjoying the beautiful views while having a picnic up here to recharge your batteries.
Picturesque section of the Viriato path (GR 63) that runs bordering Mount Peladas and Cabeza del Turmal offering wonderful views towards the Sierra de Gredos area.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Cerro Cruces mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in El Real de San Vicente. You should be able to find parking within the village, which serves as a convenient base for accessing the trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de San Vicente offers a pleasant climate for mountain biking during spring and autumn. Summers can be hot, making early morning rides advisable, while winters might bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions, especially at higher elevations like Sierra Cruces.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Sierra de San Vicente area?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking this trail or within the Sierra de San Vicente. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are often allowed on trails in this region, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the varied terrain, potential wildlife encounters, and shared use with other outdoor enthusiasts. The route can be demanding, so consider your dog's fitness for a long, difficult ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and often challenging. You'll encounter well-surfaced tracks, but also technical singletrack sections (described as "tramos de sendero" and "senda técnica"), and some paved roads with light traffic. Expect rough and rocky areas, with some sections containing "mucha piedra" (a lot of stone). Some parts are even considered "trialeras," extremely technical singletracks.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the picturesque Sierra de San Vicente, offering wide views, including the Alberche valley. You'll pass through diverse landscapes of oaks, chestnuts, and holm oaks. A key highlight is Sierra Cruces, one of the highest points in the area, providing panoramic vistas. The route also incorporates sections of the historic Senda Viriato (GR 63).

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route is part of the larger GR 63 Senda de Viriato. It also intersects with and utilizes sections of other official trails such as Senda de Viriato, Cabeza del Oso y Pelados, Mirador de Almendral de la Cañada, and Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental, among others.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is considered difficult and demanding. It features steep ascents and descents, rugged terrain, and technical sections that require very good physical condition and advanced mountain biking skills. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the demanding nature and varied terrain, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, and appropriate safety gear (helmet, gloves). A GPS device with the route loaded is highly recommended, as signage for the GR 63 can be sparse or deteriorated in some areas, making navigation challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to resupply near the trail?
faqs.answer: El Real de San Vicente, where the route starts and ends, is a village where you can find basic amenities, including cafes, pubs, and possibly small shops for resupply before or after your ride. Along the trail itself, services will be limited, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the rich and diverse landscape of the Sierra de San Vicente, often described as a "green oasis." The route passes through mountains covered with oaks, chestnuts, holm oaks, and junipers, alongside numerous streams and springs. Expect views of hills bordered by meadows, high flat areas ("navas"), and wooded pastures ("dehesas").
